description: This figure presents room-temperature engineering stress-strain curves of low-density Fe-20Mn-Al-C based alloys, consisting of two subplots: (a) displays curves for Fe-Mn-Al-C quaternary alloys, covering 5 nominal compositions: Fe-20Mn-10Al-1.5C, Fe-20Mn-13Al-0.75C, Fe-20Mn-11Al-1.0C, Fe-20Mn-10Al-1.0C, and Fe-20Mn-10Al-0.5C; (b) displays curves for Fe-Mn-Al-C-5Cr quinary alloys with 5 wt% Cr addition, covering 4 compositions: Fe-20Mn-11Al-1.8C-5Cr, Fe-20Mn-13Al-1.3C-5Cr, Fe-20Mn-12Al-1.0C-5Cr, and Fe-20Mn-10Al-1.5C-5Cr. It is observed that yield strength and ultimate tensile strength of both alloy systems increase with higher Al and C contents, some high C/Al alloys show a clear upper yield point followed by near-constant flow stress, while tensile elongation ranges from ~20% to ~60%. The results demonstrate that these Fe-20Mn-Al-C(-5Cr) alloys realize excellent strength-ductility synergy derived from nano-sized κ-carbide precipitation hardening, and possess outstanding application potential as low-density high-specific-strength structural materials.
